description_long: This object is a traditional Japanese torii gate, commonly found at the entrance to Shinto shrines. It features two robust cylindrical red pillars supporting a pair of horizontal crossbars, with the uppermost crossbar slightly curved and painted black. The structure is accented by a central rectangular plaque at the top, which often bears inscriptions or the name of the shrine. The wood appears weathered, giving it an authentic, historical look, and the black elements contrast sharply with the vibrant red. The base consists of two thick, black horizontal beams that stabilize the gate. The overall design is simple yet iconic, symbolizing the transition from the mundane to the sacred.
description: A traditional Japanese torii gate with red cylindrical pillars, black curved crossbars, and a central plaque, symbolizing a shrine entrance.
description_view_0: Front view showing both red pillars, black crossbars, and the central plaque clearly.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth of the crossbars and the cylindrical shape of the pillars.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with visible inscriptions on the central plaque and lower crossbar.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again highlighting the thickness of the crossbars and the roundness of the pillars.
